title: Webcam Supervisor
description: A walkthrough of the Webcam source and its supervisor-style reactions to posture, gaze, and expression.

steps.title: Open Vision -> Webcam
steps.body: This tutorial covers the Webcam source. If its tab is missing, first include Webcam in the Vision source selector on the Core page.

steps.title: Webcam Source
steps.body: The Webcam source provides current visual evidence about the user: posture, gaze, expression, visible props, and general energy. It is not treated as a visible user-upload unless you explicitly send an image.

steps.title: Supervisor Tab
steps.body: Now switch to the Supervisor child tab. This uses the same persona and behavior-card pattern as Screen and Clipboard, but its trigger evidence comes from the user's appearance on webcam.

steps.title: Semantic Matching
steps.body: Webcam Supervisor is intended to match your Visual Trigger semantically. If your trigger says tired, sleepy, or low-energy, webcam summaries like heavy-eyed, eyes closed, rubbing eyes, or slumped posture should all count when the evidence is clear enough.

steps.title: Avoiding Repetition
steps.body: Like the other supervisors, Webcam tries to avoid repetitive nagging by preferring new, meaningful cues over repeating the same warning every cycle.

steps.title: Best Use
steps.body: Webcam Supervisor works best for fatigue, distraction, attention, expression, mood, and presence cues that are visible enough to feel intentional instead of creepy or over-claimed.
